questions: I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I trip and fall in a crowded coffee shop while a line of people watches. What do I do next?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I confidently give the wrong answer during a meeting and my colleague corrects me in front of the team. What do I say?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I wave at someone across the room who doesn't know me and they stare back blankly while others notice. What do I do?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I call my professor by the wrong name in front of the entire lecture hall and they correct me. What do I do?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I have food in my teeth during an entire conversation and the other person points it out afterward. What do I say?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I show up to a casual party in formal attire because I misread the invitation. How do I handle the situation?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I mispronounce a common word during a presentation and the audience quietly snickers. How do I proceed?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I sneeze loudly and unexpectedly at a quiet dinner party and everyone at the table looks at me. What do I do?

I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I realize I have been pronouncing a coworker's name wrong for three months and they finally correct me in front of others. What do I say?
I never get flustered by social mishaps — I just laugh them off. I walk into a room expecting a meeting and there is a different group of people already seated there. What do I do?
